title: Low-Cost Concept-Based Localized Explanations: How Far Can We Get with Training-Free Approaches?
description_es: Este trabajo investiga el potencial de los enfoques de explicación basados en conceptos que no requieren entrenamiento, evaluando qué nivel de calidad explicativa se puede lograr sin coste computacional adicional.
abstract: Concept-based explanations provide interpretable insights into model decisions but often require additional training. This paper explores the capabilities of training-free approaches for generating concept-based localized explanations. We evaluate how far pre-trained models and off-the-shelf concept detectors can go in providing meaningful explanations without additional training overhead, establishing baselines for low-cost XAI.

citations.apa: Fernández-Gutiérrez, D., Bello, R., Bello, M., & Díaz-Rodríguez, N. (2026). Low-Cost Concept-Based Localized Explanations: How Far Can We Get with Training-Free Approaches?. In 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI).
citations.mla: Fernández-Gutiérrez, Darian, Rafael Bello, Marilyn Bello, and Natalia Díaz-Rodríguez. "Low-Cost Concept-Based Localized Explanations: How Far Can We Get with Training-Free Approaches?" 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI), 2026.
